title: Employee Standards of Conduct
description: This policy sets the behavioral and ethical standards that all Conroe ISD employees are expected to follow while on the job. It applies to every district employee, from teachers and staff to administrators, outlining what conduct is acceptable and what may result in disciplinary action. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it helps ensure that the adults working with students maintain professional, safe, and trustworthy behavior in schools.

timeline.summary: This is a cover/agenda slide for a Second Reading of nine Local board policies for Conroe ISD. The policies span topics including board governance, fiscal management, revenues, cybersecurity, employee conduct, curriculum, gifted & talented programs, and campus conduct/advertising. No redline text or specific language changes are visible on this page.

timeline.summary: This page displays the Conroe ISD local policy DH (Employee Standards of Conduct), dated 2020-02-05, Version 1. It covers employee conduct standards, violations, and weapons prohibitions on District property. No redline changes (underlined additions or strikethrough removals) are visible on this page — it appears to be a clean/final version of the policy.

### CQB Local — Technology Resources/Cyber Security
- Requires cyber security training for employees.
- Confirmed the district's cyber security coordinator is in place.

### DH Local — Employee Standards of Conduct
- Updates language regarding vaping, e-cigarettes, and nicotine products to apply to all employees and service providers (not just educators).
- Discussion about whether Zyn pouches (nicotine but no tobacco) would be covered — determined they would be as a "nicotine product."

timeline.notes: This is page 254 of the board packet, Policy Update Page Number 20. The document shows a clean (non-redlined) version of policy DH, Version 1, issued 2020-02-05. No tracked changes are visible. It is possible this is the adopted final text rather than a redline/comparison document. The policy addresses: employee accountability to Educators' Code of Ethics, violations of conduct standards, and weapons prohibition with exceptions (Board-authorized firearm use per CKE, locked vehicle storage with Texas handgun license, and District-approved supervised activities per FOD).
